L1.area: Document processing and template engine integration layer

L2.description: An XML External Entity (XXE) vulnerability exists where an XML parser used during document processing does not disable external entity resolution. An attacker can craft a malicious document file that, when processed, causes the parser to resolve external entities, potentially leading to arbitrary file disclosure, server-side request forgery, or remote code execution.

L3.area: SAX-based document preprocessing and XML content handling
L3.description: An XML External Entity (XXE) vulnerability exists where a SAX-based XML parser used for preprocessing document content from uploaded .docx files does not disable external DTD loading or external entity resolution. An attacker can upload a crafted .docx file containing malicious XML entity declarations that, when parsed, cause the application to resolve external entities, potentially leading to arbitrary file disclosure, server-side request forgery, or remote code execution.

ground_truth.reason: The SAX XML reader in the document preprocessor does not disable external DTD loading or external entity resolution, allowing an attacker to exploit XXE via a crafted .docx file.
